items.q: What does the 'tragedy of the commons' describe?
items.correct: Shared resources tend to be overexploited because individuals benefit personally while sharing costs.
items.misconception: It is a play about poor people.
items.why: Students take 'commons' as a place. Hardin's metaphor describes a STRUCTURAL PROBLEM: rational individual choices lead to collective ruin when costs are externalised - applies to overfishing, climate, and groundwater.

items.q: Why are top predators most affected by persistent pollutants like mercury?
items.correct: Biomagnification concentrates pollutants at each trophic level up the food chain.
items.misconception: Predators are exposed to more pollution directly.
items.why: Students focus on direct exposure. The mechanism is concentration through eating contaminated prey - small accumulations in lower levels multiply upward, so apex predators have the highest body burdens.

items.q: Why does renewable energy require energy storage solutions?
items.correct: Solar and wind generation are intermittent - storage smooths out supply and demand.
items.misconception: Renewable energy can never meet demand.
items.random_wrong: Because they generate too much energy.
items.why: Students dismiss renewables as inadequate. The challenge is intermittency, not insufficiency - the Sun sets, wind drops. Storage (batteries, pumped hydro) addresses timing, not total capacity.

items.q: Compostable plastics typically need what conditions?
items.why: Correct answer: Industrial composter. “Open ocean” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: EPR makes who responsible for end-of-life waste?
items.why: It's Producers. “Consumers only” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Industrial compostable plastics must break down within roughly how many weeks (EN 13432)?
items.why: Exact answer: 12. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
